request.description:
### Get Loyalty Tier Details

This endpoint retrieves the details of the loyalty tier including tier ID, tier name, background colors, remaining points, progress percentage, and details of the next tier if available.

#### Response

- **message** (string, optional): A message related to the response.
- **data** (object or array): An object containing the details of the loyalty tier, including:
    - **id** (string): The ID of the loyalty tier.
    - **tier** (string): The tier level.
    - **tier_name** (string): The name of the tier.
    - **bg_color_first** (string): The first background color associated with the tier.
    - **bg_color_second** (string): The second background color associated with the tier.
    - **remaining_points** (string): The remaining points required to reach the next tier.
    - **remaining_point_text** (string): Text related to the remaining points.
    - **progress_percentage** (string): The progress percentage towards the next tier.
    - **next_tier_id** (string, optional): The ID of the next tier.
    - **next_tier** (string, optional): The next tier level.
    - **next_tier_name** (string, optional): The name of the next tier.

#### Example Response

``` json
{
    "message": "",
    "data": {
        "id": "",
        "tier": "",
        "tier_name": "",
        "bg_color_first": "",
        "bg_color_second": "",
        "remaining_points": "",
        "remaining_point_text": "",
        "progress_percentage": "",
        "next_tier_id": "",
        "next_tier": "",
        "next_tier_name": ""
    }
}

 ```

    - **bg_color_first** (string): The first background color associated with the tier.
    - **bg_color_second** (string): The second background color associated with the tier.
    - **remaining_points** (string): The remaining points required to reach the next tier.
    - **remaining_point_text** (string): Text related to the remaining points.
    - **progress_percentage** (string): The progress percentage towards the next tier.
    - **next_tier_id** (string, optional): The ID of the next tier.
    - **next_tier** (string, optional): The next tier level.
    - **next_tier_name** (string, optional): The name of the next tier.

#### Example Response

``` json
{
    "message": "",
    "data": {
        "id": "",
        "tier": "",
        "tier_name": "",
        "bg_color_first": "",
        "bg_color_second": "",
        "remaining_points": "",
        "remaining_point_text": "",
        "progress_percentage": "",
        "next_tier_id": "",
        "next_tier": "",
        "next_tier_name": ""
    }
}

 ```

response.name: Get Teams Member

request.description:
### Purpose

This API endpoint is used to add a new team member with their details.

### Request Body

- `first_name` (text, required, length:100): The first name of the team member.
    
- `last_name` (text, required, length:100): The last name of the team member.
    
- `mobile_number` (text, required, length:10): The mobile number of the team member.
    
- `opus_pc_id` (text, optional): The ID related to the team member.
    

### Response

The response will have a status code of 200 and a JSON content type. It will contain the following structure:

``` json
{
    "message": "string",
    "data": {
        "id": 0,
        "first_name": "string",
        "last_name": "string",
        "mobile_number": "string",
        "type": "string",
        "is_primary": "string",
        "image": "string",
        "popup_content": {
            "popup_title": "string",
            "popup_description": "string"
        }
    }
}

 ```

The `message` field contains any additional information, and the `data` field provides details of the newly added team member, including their ID, name, mobile number, type, primary status, image, and popup content.

### Example

``` json
{
    "message": "",
    "data": {
        "id": 0,
        "first_name": "",
        "last_name": "",
        "mobile_number": "",
        "type": "",
        "is_primary": "",
        "image": "",
        "popup_content": {
            "popup_title": "",
            "popup_description": ""
        }
    }
}

 ```

request.description:
### GET /pending_team_requests

This endpoint retrieves pending team requests.

#### Request

- Method: GET
    
- URL: {{BASE_URL}}/pending_team_requests
    
- Headers:
    - Content-Type: application/json
        
    - Authorization: Bearer
        

##### Request Body

``` json
{
  "search_key": ""
}

 ```

#### Response

The response for this request is a JSON object with the following schema:

``` json
{
  "message": "string",
  "data": [
    {
      "id": "number",
      "first_name": "string",
      "last_name": "string",
      "mobile_number": "string",
      "type": "string",
      "image": "string"
    }
  ]
}

 ```

The `message` field contains a string indicating the status of the request, while the `data` field is an array of objects, each representing a pending team request. Each object in the `data` array includes the `id`, `first_name`, `last_name`, `mobile_number`, `type`, and `image` of the team request.

request.description:
### POST /dealer

This endpoint is used to create a new dealer.

#### Request Body

- `dealer_shop_name` (string, required): The name of the dealer's shop.
    
- `dealer_code` (string, required): The code assigned to the dealer.
    
- `mobile_number` (string, required): The mobile number of the dealer.
    

#### Response

- Status: 427
    
- Content-Type: application/json
    

``` json
{
    "message": "string",
    "errors": {}
}

 ```

The response message will contain a string indicating the status of the request, and any errors will be provided in the 'errors' object.

request.description:
1. `'qr_codes' => ['required', 'array', 'min:1', 'max:10']`: This rule ensures that the input field `qr_codes` is required, must be an array, and must contain at least 1 element and at most 10 elements.
    
2. `'qr_codes.\*' => ['required', 'string', 'min:12']`: Here, the `\*` wildcard notation applies validation rules to each element within the `qr_codes` array individually. So, it requires each element to be a string and have a minimum length of 12 characters.
    
3. `'latitude' => ['nullable', 'numeric']`: This rule specifies that the `latitude` field is optional (`nullable`) and must be a numeric value.
    
4. `'longitude' => ['nullable', 'numeric']`: Similar to latitude, this rule makes the `longitude` field optional and ensures it's a numeric value.
    
5. `'pincode' => ['required', 'integer', 'digits:6']`: The `pincode` field is required and must be an integer with exactly 6 digits.
    
6. `'scan_method' => ['required', 'in:C,M,G']`: This rule states that the `scan_method` field is required and must have a value that is one of 'C', 'M', or 'G'.
    
7. `'scan_images' => ['required_unless:scan_method,M']`: Here, `scan_images` is required unless the value of `scan_method` is 'M'. This means that if the `scan_method` is not 'M', at least one `scan_images` file must be provided.
    
8. `'scan_images.\*' => ['mimes:jpg,jpeg,png,bmp,svg,webp']`: Finally, for each element within the `scan_images` array, the rule ensures that each file uploaded has one of the specified MIME types: jpg, jpeg, png, bmp, svg, or webp.

request.description:
'qr_code' => \['required', 'string', 'min:12'\],           

'latitude' => \['nullable', 'numeric'\],           

'longitude' => \['nullable', 'numeric'\],           

'pincode' => \['required', 'integer', 'digits:6'\],            'scan_method' => \['required', 'in:C,M,O,OM'\], //C:camera, M:manual, O:OCR, OM:OCR Manual           

'scan_image' => \['required_if:scan_method,C','mimes:jpg,jpeg,png,bmp,svg,webp'\]

request.description:
This endpoint makes an HTTP GET request to retrieve a list of transactions. The request includes query parameters for page number, sorting criteria, number of transactions per page, and date range for filtering. The response returns a status code of 200 along with a JSON object containing a message, transaction data array, total number of transactions, and other metadata related to the transactions.

The transaction data array includes details such as channel ID, coupon code, status, category ID, product code, creation timestamp, payment method, point credit/debit information, reference type, product and category names, mobile number, transaction ID, and more.

The user should refer to the response data structure to understand the details of each transaction and the associated metadata.

request.description:
1. `'qr_codes' => ['required', 'array', 'min:1', 'max:10']`: This rule ensures that the input field `qr_codes` is required, must be an array, and must contain at least 1 element and at most 10 elements.
    
2. `'qr_codes.\*' => ['required', 'string', 'min:12']`: Here, the `\*` wildcard notation applies validation rules to each element within the `qr_codes` array individually. So, it requires each element to be a string and have a minimum length of 12 characters.
    
3. `'latitude' => ['nullable', 'numeric']`: This rule specifies that the `latitude` field is optional (`nullable`) and must be a numeric value.
    
4. `'longitude' => ['nullable', 'numeric']`: Similar to latitude, this rule makes the `longitude` field optional and ensures it's a numeric value.
    
5. `'pincode' => ['required', 'integer', 'digits:6']`: The `pincode` field is required and must be an integer with exactly 6 digits.
    
6. `'scan_method' => ['required', 'in:C,M,G']`: This rule states that the `scan_method` field is required and must have a value that is one of 'C', 'M', or 'G'.
    
7. `'scan_images' => ['required_unless:scan_method,M']`: Here, `scan_images` is required unless the value of `scan_method` is 'M'. This means that if the `scan_method` is not 'M', at least one `scan_images` file must be provided.
    
8. `'scan_images.\*' => ['mimes:jpg,jpeg,png,bmp,svg,webp']`: Finally, for each element within the `scan_images` array, the rule ensures that each file uploaded has one of the specified MIME types: jpg, jpeg, png, bmp, svg, or webp.

request.description:
This API used to transfer the cash into bank or UPI account as per selection of payment type

\[M\] Mandatory Field,  
\[NM\] Non-Mandatory Field

otp: \[M\]\[6 digit only\]  
points : \[M\] \[integer\] \[Min threshold points is 1000\]  
payment_type: \[M\] \[ B - Bank, U - UPI\]  
pc_bank_id: \[NM\] \[M - If payment type is B\]  
pc_upi_id: \[NM\] \[M - If payment type is U\]  
latitude : \[NM\] \[decimal form\]\[Like, 14.585655\] - \[M\] if request comes from Mobile App  
longitde : \[NM\] \[decimal form\]\[Like, 14.585655\] - \[M\] if request comes from Mobile App

Status:

```
'status' => [
            'key' => [
                'pending'    => 'P',
                'success'    => 'S',
                'fail'       => 'F',
                'rejected'   => 'R',
                'processing' => 'O',
                'reversed'   => 'X',
            ],
            'value' => [
                'pending'    => 'Pending',
                'success'    => 'Success',
                'fail'       => 'Fail',
                'rejected'   => 'Rejected',
                'processing' => 'Processing',
                'reversed'   => 'Reversed',
            ],
 ],

 ```

request.description: This endpoint retrieves cash transfer details. The response will include various parameters such as factor, cash transfer limit, compliance status, TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) details, total redemption, compliance limit, TDS applicable amount, and a list of TDS text messages. The "message" field in the response may contain additional information.

Cash Transfer Details - Response Updated

request.description:
This HTTP GET request is used to send a redemption OTP. The request does not require any specific parameters. Upon successful execution, the API returns a JSON response with a status code of 200 and a content type of application/json. The response includes a message field and a data field, both of which are empty arrays.

Example response:

``` json
{
    "message":"",
    "data":[]
}

 ```

query.description: I: Interior, E: Exterior, IE: Interior+Exterior
D: Draft, A: Acitve, N: Not applicable, P: Pending
Filter when when for product_tag project list get

request.description:
This endpoint makes an HTTP GET request to retrieve project details with pagination, sorting, and filtering options. The request includes parameters for page number, items per page, sorting order, search keyword, project status, and site type.

### Request Body

The request does not include a request body.

### Response

The response returns a status code of 200 and a JSON object with a message and data array. The data array contains project details such as ID, project name, customer contact details, address, lead type, requirements, paintable area, architect information, rooms count, warranty status, and site status.

Example Response:

``` json
{
    "message": "",
    "data": [
        {
            "id": 0,
            "project_name": "",
            "customer_mobile": "",
            "customer_email": "",
            "first_name": "",
            "last_name": "",
            "address1": "",
            "street": "",
            "city": "",
            "state": "",
            "pincode": "",
            "lead_type": "",
            "lead_type_view_text": "",
            "lead_type_icon_text": "",
            "lead_date": "",
            "requirement": [
                {
                    "title": "",
                    "value": "",
                    "icon": ""
                }
            ],
            "total_paintable_area": null,
            "is_architect": "",
            "architect_name": null,
            "architect_mobile": "",
            "architect_opus_pc_id": null,
            "no_of_rooms": 0,
            "warranty_registered": "",
            "status": "",
            "warranty_status": "",
            "state_name": "",
            "expected_end_date": "",
            "site_status": "",
            "site_status_name": "",
            "site_start_date": "",
            "site_close_date": "",
            "site_verification_date": ""
        }
    ]
}

 ```

request.description:
### Create Project

This endpoint allows the mobile team to create a new project or update an existing one. The request should be sent as an HTTP POST to `{{BASE_URL}}/create-project`.

#### Request Body

- `project_id` (integer): If null, new records will be created. If provided, the mobile team must pass the ID for updating the project.
    
- `project_name` (string, required, max length 50): The name of the project.
    
- `customer_name` (string, required, max length 50): The name of the customer.
    
- `customer_mobile` (string, required, length 10): The mobile number of the customer.
    
- `email` (string, max length 50): The email address of the customer.
    
- `address_line_1` (string, required, max length 100): The first line of the customer's address.
    
- `address_line_2` (string, required, max length 100): The second line of the customer's address.
    
- `pincode` (string, required, length 6): The pin code of the customer's address.
    
- `no_of_rooms` (integer): The number of rooms in the project.
    
- `is_architect` (string, required, length 1, values: Y/N): Indicates if there is an architect involved.
    
- `architect_name` (string, max length 50): The name of the architect.
    
- `architect_mobile` (string, length 10): The mobile number of the architect.
    
- `architect_opus_id` (string, length 10): The OPUS ID of the architect.
    
- `lead_type` (string, values: I/E/IE): Indicates the type of lead - Interior, Exterior, or both.
    
- `requirement` (array): An array of requirements for the project.
    
- `customer_confirmation` (boolean): If true, an OTP will be sent to the customer's mobile.
    

#### Response

- `message` (string): A message related to the request.
    
- `data` (object): Information about the project.
    
- `otp_sent` (boolean): Indicates if an OTP was sent to the customer's mobile.
    

An example response:

``` json
{
    "message": "",
    "data": {
        "id": 0,
        "project_name": "",
        "opus_pc_id": "",
        "customer_mobile": "",
        "customer_email": "",
        "customer_name": "",
        "no_of_rooms": 0,
        "site_status": "",
        "total_paintable_area": null,
        "total_paintable_area_unit": null,
        "address1": "",

        "source": "",
        "status": "",
        "deleted_at": null,
        "created_at": "",
        "updated_at": ""
    },
    "otp_sent": true
}

 ```

request.description:
This endpoint allows you to create a new warranty product. The request should be sent as an HTTP POST to {{BASE_URL}}/create-warranty-product.

### Request Body

The request body should be in JSON format and include the following parameters:

- `warranty_id` (integer, required): The ID of the warranty for the product.
    
- `product_code` (string, required, max length: 20): The code of the product (up to 20 characters).
    
- `pack_code` (string, required, max length: 20): The code of the product pack (up to 20 characters).
    

### Response

The response will include the details of the newly created warranty product.

request.description:
Scan expo event QR code to mark attendance and earn points.

**Authentication Required:** Bearer token for authenticated Painter/Contractor

**Request Body:**
- `event_qr_code` (required, string): QR code from expo event (10 or 18 characters)
- `latitude` (optional, numeric): User's current latitude
- `longitude` (optional, numeric): User's current longitude

**Response:**
- Success: Returns attendance record with earned points
- Error: Returns error message with details

**User Types Allowed:** P (Painter), C (Contractor), TC (Technical Contractor)

response.name: Success - Scan Expo Event Code
Negative - Unauthenticated
Negative - Required Field
Negative - Invalid QR
Negative - QR Size Validation
Negative - Cancelled Event Restriction
Negative - Scheduled Date Validation

request.description:
## How It Works

This endpoint makes an HTTP GET request to retrieve information about how the system works.

### Request

No request body is required for this endpoint.

### Response

The response will be a JSON object with the following schema:

```json
{
    "message": "string",
    "data": [
        {
            "id": "number",
            "title": "string",
            "description": "string",
            "icon": "string"
        }
    ]
}
```
The `message` field contains a string message, and the `data` field is an array of objects, each containing an `id`, `title`, `description`, and `icon`.

query parameters:

StartFragmentpage

sort_by : sorting on table field e.g created_at.

sort_order : asc or decs

year : year filter on created at

status : code history status

reference_type : point history reference type e.g

C = Code History,

T = Cash Transfer,

P = Product Purchase,

E = Extra Points,

D = Points Deduction,

M = Milestone Points,

B = Cash Bonus Points,

L = Loyalty Bonus Points,

A = Auto Redeem,

X = Exception Points

This API used to transfer the cash into bank or UPI account usign set default payment method

\[M\] Mandatory Field,  
\[NM\] Non-Mandatory Field

points : \[M\] \[integer\] \[Min threshold points is 1000\]  
latitude : \[NM\] \[decimal form\]\[Like, 14.585655\] - \[M\] if request comes from Mobile App  
longitde : \[NM\] \[decimal form\]\[Like, 14.585655\] - \[M\] if request comes from Mobile App

Status:

```
'status' => [
            'key' => [
                'pending'    => 'P',
                'success'    => 'S',
                'fail'       => 'F',
                'rejected'   => 'R',
                'processing' => 'O',
                'reversed'   => 'X',
            ],
            'value' => [
                'pending'    => 'Pending',
                'success'    => 'Success',
                'fail'       => 'Fail',
                'rejected'   => 'Rejected',
                'processing' => 'Processing',
                'reversed'   => 'Reversed',
            ],
 ],

 ```

```
gst_number: [M][max][15]
Regular expression validation on server side is
/^(\d{2}[A-Z]{5}\d{4}[A-Z]{1}[1-9A-Z]{1}[Z]{1}[1-9A-Z]{1})|(\d{2}[A-Z]{4}\d{5}[A-Z]{1}[1-9A-Z]{1}[Z]{1}[1-9A-Z]{1})|(\d{2}[A-Z]{3}\d{6}[A-Z]{1}[1-9A-Z]{1}[Z]{1}[1-9A-Z]{1})$/
Notes:
^: Start of the string.
\d{2}: Match two digits at the beginning (indicating the state code).
[A-Z]{5}: Match five uppercase letters (indicating the PAN number).
\d{4}: Match four digits.
[A-Z]{1}: Match one uppercase letter (indicating the entity type).
[1-9A-Z]{1}: Match one alphanumeric character excluding '0' (indicating the entity code).
[Z]{1}: Match 'Z'.
[1-9A-Z]{1}: Match one alphanumeric character excluding '0' (indicating the checksum).
|: OR operator to handle different GST formats.
Repeat similar patterns for other possible GST formats.

 ```

If this parameter is not provided, all data will be retrieved.

page=1&item_count=2

request.description:
StartFragment

**Status: I | Reference ID: 209 | Type: 1**

The possible values for `status` are as follows:

- **I**: Initiated
    
- **A**: Assigned
    
- **R**: In Review
    
- **C**: Closed
    

Status logic:

- If you pass **I**, only the "Initiated" status will be filled.
    
- If you pass **A**, both "Initiated" and "Assigned" statuses will be filled.
    
- If you pass **R**, the statuses "Initiated," "Assigned," and "In Review" will be filled.
    
- If you pass **C**, all statuses ("Initiated," "Assigned," "In Review," and "Closed") will be filled.
    

The key name is `is_fillable`, and the possible values are `true` or `false`.

Additionally, you need to provide `reference_id=209` and `type=1` to retrieve single transaction details. The `type` is used to specify whether to fetch from code history or cash transfer.

We are not querying the complaint table. Please retrieve the detailed complaint information using the "Get All Complaints" API and ensure that you pass all necessary data into the API screen.

request.description: Use a mobile mapped to institutional contractor. Expects messages.app_login.no_access.

request.description: Requires DB setup: block_status=auto_block, block_through=otp. Expects error with is_auto_blocked=true.

Requires DB setup: block_status=manual_block. Expects messages.app_login.manual_blocked_account.
Requires DB setup: status=delete. Expects messages.app_login.inactive_account.

request.description: Simulate via multiple calls or throttle config: RateLimiter ip_restrication. Expects messages.rate_limiting_message.ip.

item.name: Send OTP - Rate limit exceeded by mobile number

request.description: Simulate via more than 3 calls per day to same mobile: mobile_restrication. Expects messages.rate_limiting_message.mobile_number.

item.name: Send OTP - Per-minute OTP throttle exceeded

request.description: RateLimiter::attempt('OT'+mobile) fails within minute. Expects messages.rate_limiting_message.otp.
Main URL for send-otp. Requires: mobile_number. Mobile must exist, not blocked/deleted, not institutional contractor. Subject to rate limits.

item.name: Resend OTP - Rate limit exceeded by mobile number

request.description: Expects messages.rate_limiting_message.mobile_number when threshold exceeded.
Main URL for resend-otp. Same validations as send-otp (same controller method).

response.name: Main - resend-otp

Validation fail: integer.

request.description: Validation fail: digits:10.
Validation fail: otp required.

item.name: Verify OTP - Wrong length otp (not 6 digits)

request.description: Validation fail: digits:6.
Expects messages.app_login.invalid_mobile_otp (422).
Set OTP older than 10 minutes in DB to test. Expects messages.app_login.invalid_mobile_otp or expired variant depending on logic; controller returns invalid if not found by date constraint.

item.name: Verify OTP - OAuth client not found

request.description: Requires env where no Passport password_client exists. Expects messages.app_login.oauth_client_not_found.
Main URL for verify-otp. Requires: mobileNo (digits:10, integer), otp (digits:6).

response.name: Main - verify-otp

            {
                "question": "When does Colour Stars officially start?",
                "answer": "It officially begins from 1st April 2026."
            },
            {
                "question": "What is Fast Track?",
                "answer": "A one-time qualification window (1st December 2025 – 31st March 2026) to secure your Colour Stars tier early."
            },
            {
                "question": "Is 50,000 points compulsory?",
                "answer": "Yes — to qualify this year, you need to earn 50,000 points from 1st December 2025 to 31st March 2026."
            },
            {
                "question": "What if I don't reach 50,000 points?",
                "answer": "You can still qualify next year based on your performance from 1st April 2026 to 31st March 2027. You would be able to enjoy the benefits of the Colour Stars Loyalty Club from April 2027 (FY 2027–28)."
            },
            {
                "question": "What is the 15% bonus for?",
                "answer": "To reward consistent past performance and help reach higher tiers."
            },
            {
                "question": "Can bonus points be redeemed as cash?",
                "answer": "No. Bonus points are only for tier calculation."
            },
            {
                "question": "If I redeem my points, will my tier reduce?",
                "answer": "No. Tier depends on points earned, not points balance."
            },
            {
                "question": "Are the rewards earned within 1st December '25 to 31st March '26 part of next year's program?",
                "answer": "No, they are special Fast Track rewards only. Basis the earnings next year, you'll be eligible for the rewards in the upcoming year."
            },
            {
                "question": "When will next year's Colour Stars rewards be announced?",
                "answer": "They will be announced later and will depend entirely on your tier."
            },
            {
                "question": "How do I get help?",

            }

        "faqs": [
            {
                "question": "When does Colour Stars officially start?",
                "answer": "It officially begins from 1st April 2026."
            },
            {
                "question": "What is Fast Track?",
                "answer": "A one-time qualification window (1st December 2025 – 31st March 2026) to secure your Colour Stars tier early."
            },
            {
                "question": "Is 50,000 points compulsory?",
                "answer": "Yes — to qualify this year, you need to earn 50,000 points from 1st December 2025 to 31st March 2026."
            },
            {
                "question": "What if I don't reach 50,000 points?",
                "answer": "You can still qualify next year based on your performance from 1st April 2026 to 31st March 2027. You would be able to enjoy the benefits of the Colour Stars Loyalty Club from April 2027 (FY 2027–28)."
            },
            {
                "question": "What is the 15% bonus for?",
                "answer": "To reward consistent past performance and help reach higher tiers."
            },
            {
                "question": "Can bonus points be redeemed as cash?",
                "answer": "No. Bonus points are only for tier calculation."
            },
            {
                "question": "If I redeem my points, will my tier reduce?",
                "answer": "No. Tier depends on points earned, not points balance."
            },
            {
                "question": "Are the rewards earned within 1st December '25 to 31st March '26 part of next year's program?",
                "answer": "No, they are special Fast Track rewards only. Basis the earnings next year, you'll be eligible for the rewards in the upcoming year."
            },
            {
                "question": "When will next year's Colour Stars rewards be announced?",
                "answer": "They will be announced later and will depend entirely on your tier."
            },
            {
                "question": "How do I get help?",

            }

        "faqs": [
            {
                "question": "When does Colour Stars officially start?",
                "answer": "It officially begins from 1st April 2026."
            },
            {
                "question": "What is Fast Track?",
                "answer": "A one-time qualification window (1st December 2025 – 31st March 2026) to secure your Colour Stars tier early."
            },
            {
                "question": "Is 50,000 points compulsory?",
                "answer": "Yes — to qualify this year, you need to earn 50,000 points from 1st December 2025 to 31st March 2026."
            },
            {
                "question": "What if I don't reach 50,000 points?",
                "answer": "You can still qualify next year based on your performance from 1st April 2026 to 31st March 2027. You would be able to enjoy the benefits of the Colour Stars Loyalty Club from April 2027 (FY 2027–28)."
            },
            {
                "question": "What is the 15% bonus for?",
                "answer": "To reward consistent past performance and help reach higher tiers."
            },
            {
                "question": "Can bonus points be redeemed as cash?",
                "answer": "No. Bonus points are only for tier calculation."
            },
            {
                "question": "If I redeem my points, will my tier reduce?",
                "answer": "No. Tier depends on points earned, not points balance."
            },
            {
                "question": "Are the rewards earned within 1st December '25 to 31st March '26 part of next year's program?",
                "answer": "No, they are special Fast Track rewards only. Basis the earnings next year, you'll be eligible for the rewards in the upcoming year."
            },
            {
                "question": "When will next year's Colour Stars rewards be announced?",
                "answer": "They will be announced later and will depend entirely on your tier."
            },

                "question": "How do I get help?",
